region_of_origin: United States (sold in Singapore via Dunkin' Donuts)
food_subgroup: Yeast-raised glazed donut with mocha-flavoured icing and decorative toppings

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for bear-shaped mocha character donut; closest parent 'doughnut food product' (FOODON_00002187) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX (Doughnuts) is the closest parent category; no specific code for mocha-flavoured character donuts exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific novelty donut product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One bear-shaped mocha donut weighing approximately 75 g, as typically served at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Cake-type doughnut, plain (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB 18248); adapted with mocha-glazed yeast donut values from USDA Branded Foods reference for glazed yeast donuts
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct nutritional entry exists for Dunkin' Donuts Singapore 'Mocha the Bear' in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for yeast-raised glazed doughnuts (FDC ID 172757, 'Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, glazed, enriched') and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Foods entries for mocha-glazed donuts. Per-100g values are derived from these references. Serving size of 75 g is an estimate based on typical Dunkin' Donuts standard donut weight.

gi_source.title: Doughnut, cake-type — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; yeast-raised glazed donut analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI values for doughnuts in the range of 75–76 (cake-type doughnut, GI=76, per Foster-Powell et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2002). No specific GI entry exists for a mocha-glazed yeast bear-shaped donut. GI=76 is used as the closest analogous estimate for a glazed enriched-flour donut with sugar icing. Glycemic load per serving = (76 × 37.1 g available carbohydrate per 75 g serving) / 100 = 28.2, rounded to 28.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Each 75 g serving provides approximately 255 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption and account for this within their daily sodium budget of under 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (76) and high glycemic load (~28) per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 76 and a glycemic load of approximately 28 per serving, this donut causes a rapid and substantial postprandial blood glucose rise and should be avoided or strictly limited by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and high sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each serving contains approximately 6.2 g saturated fat and 0.2 g trans fat alongside 16.5 g sugar, all of which contribute to atherogenic risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence only.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mocha the Bear (also known as Mocha Bear Donut) is a novelty bear-shaped yeast-raised glazed donut sold at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore, listed on field survey at SGD 25.90. The donut is deep-fried from enriched wheat flour dough, then decorated with mocha-flavoured icing and food-safe colouring to resemble a bear character, making it a visually appealing treat popular with children and novelty food enthusiasts. The texture is soft and pillowy inside with a slightly crisp glazed exterior; the flavour profile combines the sweetness of sugar glaze with the bitter-sweet notes of mocha (coffee and cocoa). Nutritionally, it is an ultra-processed, energy-dense snack providing approximately 391 kcal per 100 g, with significant contributions from refined carbohydrates (49.5 g/100 g), fat (19.5 g/100 g including 8.2 g saturated fat), and sugar (22 g/100 g). The glycemic index is high (GI=76), making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing blood glucose. The sodium content (~340 mg/100 g) is moderate and relevant for hypertension management. It contains gluten, eggs, milk, and soy allergens. Halal-certified at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore outlets. Best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary item.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional data for Dunkin' Donuts Singapore 'Mocha the Bear' exists in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Branded Foods entries for yeast-raised glazed donuts, which represent the closest defensible analogous product. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cake-type doughnuts (GI=76). Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 172757, yeast-leavened glazed doughnut) as no direct approved-source entry exists for this specific Dunkin' Donuts Singapore product. 2. Serving size of 75 g is an estimate based on typical Dunkin' Donuts standard donut weight; actual product weight may vary. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy reference used. 4. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary adapted source. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary adapted source. 6. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the primary adapted source. 7. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary adapted source. 8. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary adapted source. 9. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary adapted source. 10. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero as donuts contain no vitamin C-contributing ingredients per USDA reference. 11. GI=76 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (cake-type doughnut); no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. 12. Halal status reflects Dunkin' Donuts Singapore's general halal certification; individual outlet certification should be verified. 13. Price SGD 25.90 is preserved from field survey source record (input data); this may reflect a set/bundle price rather than a single donut price. 14. Multilingual names provided for Singapore context (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the product is sold in Singapore.
